dependencies: Reliance on third-party providers for technology, information security, funds transfers, business process management, and administration
Dependence on vendors for software and system infrastructure
Cloud service providers for data hosting
Third-party asset managers and custodians for investment services
Correspondents, agents, brokers, intermediaries, insurance markets, data providers, plan trustees, payroll service providers
risks: Third-party vendors may act or fail to act in ways that could harm business
Vendor failures could disrupt operations and impact ability to offer services
Vendor non-compliance with legal, regulatory or contractual obligations
Vendor financial difficulties or service interruptions
Dependence on key vendors for strategic initiatives
Risk of compromise through supply chain attacks (e.g., CrowdStrike event July 2024)
Limited control over cybersecurity practices of third-party vendors

regulatory_risks: Extensive regulation under U.S. federal and state laws, U.K., E.U. and other jurisdictions
Trade sanctions laws (Afghanistan, Belarus, Cuba, Iran, North Korea, Russia, Syria, Ukraine, Venezuela)
Anti-corruption laws (U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act, U.K. Bribery Act)
Data privacy and protection laws (GDPR, CCPA, Australia's CPS 234)
Cybersecurity laws and regulations
Emerging AI-related laws (EU's AI Act, California AI Transparency Act, Colorado AI Act)
Financial services regulations (SEC, FINRA, FCA, Central Bank of Ireland)
Insurance licensing and regulatory requirements across jurisdictions
ERISA guidelines for pension plans
Healthcare industry regulations (Department of Health and Human Services)
FATCA regulations
Tax law changes and OECD international tax framework (Pillar Two minimum tax)
Transfer pricing regulations
Potential changes in U.S. regulatory priorities following presidential administration changes
New and rapidly evolving trade sanctions from geopolitical conflicts
Pharmacy benefit management regulations at federal and state level
State-level privacy laws (over 20 states with enacted laws)
Cybersecurity breach notification requirements (all 50 U.S. states)
Data localization requirements (China, Saudi Arabia)
U.K. tax authority examinations and assessments
Potential FTC rule on noncompete clauses
ESG and sustainability reporting requirements (U.S., U.K., E.U., Australia)
Greenwashing scrutiny from regulators
Conflicts of interest regulations for intermediaries
Contingent commission regulations
Investment adviser regulations
Pension plan regulations across jurisdictions

sanction_risks: Trade sanctions relating to Russia, Ukraine, Iran, North Korea, Syria, Venezuela, Cuba, Belarus, Afghanistan
Potential new sanctions from geopolitical conflicts (war in Ukraine, Middle East conflict)
Sanctions compliance costs and operational limitations
